mnemonics: PENNY MATCH: Players seek to outguess each other like matching pennies heads vs tails
HEADS-TAILS PAYOFF: Row wins if match (Heads-Heads or Tails-Tails), Column wins if mismatch (Heads-Tails or Tails-Heads)
keyPoints: Matching Pennies is a two-player zero-sum game with no pure strategy Nash equilibrium
Players must use mixed strategies to achieve equilibrium (each player randomizes 50/50 between Heads and Tails)
The unique mixed strategy Nash equilibrium gives each player an expected payoff of zero
commonMistakes: Assuming there is a pure strategy solution - there isn't one in Matching Pennies
Thinking players should always choose the same action - randomization is necessary for equilibrium
Believing one player has an inherent advantage - the game is perfectly symmetric and zero-sum
